# COMP110 Data Analysis Project

## Summary

In this project, I analyzed whether pre-lecture videos would improve student learning in COMP110. I examined survey data to understand student interest and how it relates to difficulty and understanding of the course.

## Visualization 1

<img src="/comp110-project/static/imgs/graph1.png" width="500"/>

This graph shows the distribution of student responses regarding pre-lecture videos. Many students selected higher values, indicating strong interest in having pre-lecture videos available.

## Visualization 2

<img src="/comp110-project/static/imgs/graph2.png" width="500"/>

This graph compares course difficulty with interest in pre-lecture videos. Students who reported higher difficulty tended to show more interest in pre-lecture videos.

## Visualization 3

<img src="/comp110-project/static/imgs/graph3.png" width="500"/>

This graph compares student understanding with interest in pre-lecture videos. Students with lower understanding levels showed greater interest in additional resources like pre-lecture videos.

## Conclusion

The data suggests that pre-lecture videos would be a valuable addition to the course. Students who struggle more with the material are especially interested in this feature. While creating videos may require additional effort from instructors, the potential benefits in improving student understanding make it a worthwhile change to consider.
